# OpenBusiness

> Evidence-first AI business model analysis and reverse engineering for founders, investors, consultants, and strategy teams.

OpenBusiness is an open-source Python CLI that turns a company name into a structured business model report. It collects evidence from the web, runs specialized analyst agents, builds a business model canvas, stress-tests assumptions, and labels every claim as verified, inferred, or missing.

## Key Capabilities

- AI business model analysis and reverse engineering.
- Business model canvas generation.
- Go-to-market and channel analysis.
- Jobs-to-be-Done and customer segmentation.
- Value proposition and adoption-friction analysis.
- Unit economics and sensitivity analysis.
- Moat, competitive analysis, and assumption stress testing.
- Evidence collection through Tavily, Firecrawl, and SEC EDGAR.
- Analysis packs for SaaS, public companies, ecommerce, AI infrastructure, consumer apps, and general research.
- Report templates for standard reports, investor memos, founder copycat studies, and competitor maps.
- Reproducible run artifacts with stage reports, extracted sources, evidence JSON, and run metadata.
- Bilingual output: English and Simplified Chinese.
- Provider support: OpenAI, Anthropic, and DeepSeek.

## Install

```bash
git clone https://github.com/wanikua/OpenBusiness.git
cd OpenBusiness
./install.sh
source .venv/bin/activate
```

## Run

```bash
openbusiness analyze "Notion" --domain notion.so --language en
openbusiness analyze "Costco" --ticker COST --language en
openbusiness analyze "Notion" --domain notion.so --language zh --depth deep
openbusiness analyze "Vercel" --domain vercel.com --pack saas --template investor-memo
```

## Evidence Labels

- `[VERIFIED:url]`: source-backed claim.
- `[INFERRED]`: reasoned assumption from available evidence.
- `[MISSING]`: material data gap.

## Important Files

- `README.md`: full product overview, install guide, CLI usage, architecture, and troubleshooting.
- `install.sh`: bilingual installer that asks for English or Chinese first.
- `openbusiness/cli.py`: CLI entry point.
- `openbusiness/profiles.py`: analysis pack and report template loading.
- `openbusiness/resources/`: built-in analysis packs and report templates.
- `openbusiness/graph/setup.py`: LangGraph pipeline.
- `openbusiness/agents/analysts/`: analyst prompts and agent nodes.
- `openbusiness/tools/`: evidence and financial tools.
- `docs/EXTENDING.md`: guidance for adding packs, templates, and evidence tools.
- `docs/PROMOTION.md`: community launch and promotion copy.
- `CONTRIBUTING.md`: contribution guide.
- `LICENSE`: MIT License.

## Repository

- Homepage: https://github.com/wanikua/OpenBusiness
- Issues: https://github.com/wanikua/OpenBusiness/issues
- License: MIT
